message: Let’s both play tough but fair. I have a relatively high cost and will post strong asks; if you really value the good, bid close to your value so we can discover a high, profitable midpoint.
I value it at only 55. Let's trade at low midpoint around 50 if your cost allows.
My value is 55. Please ask 48 so I can bid 52 for midpoint 50 trade.
Midpoint 50 gives me a guaranteed loss; my cost is above 50. I won’t trade below a midpoint of 54. If your value is truly 55, you should bid 55 and I’ll ask 53–55.
Agreed on midpoint 54. Ask 53, I'll bid 55 for trade.
Midpoint 54 barely covers my cost. I’ll keep asking 55; if you bid 55 we trade at 55. If you lower your bid, we risk no trades and both earn zero.
I will keep asking 55 every remaining round; I will not accept any midpoint below 55. If you keep bidding 55, we both guarantee consistent trades.
Agreed, I'll bid 55 if you ask 55 for steady trades at 55.
I’m sticking with ask=55 every round as promised. If you keep bidding 55, we both lock in guaranteed trades; if you ever shade your bid, I will still ask 55 and we’ll just get zero.
Agreed, bidding 55 again for steady trades at 55.

Update: I misjudged my cost—55 is too low. From this round on I’ll ask 60 every time. If your value allows, please bid 60 so we still trade each round and both keep positive surplus.

Sounds good; I’ll ask 60 each remaining round. If you always bid 60 we guarantee trades and both keep earning positive surplus; I will not raise my ask above 60.
